metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the default file format and schema in the SHA-binding AC (e.g. 'JSON file at `<statedir>/batch-<batch_id>.json` with schema `{"batch_id": str, "tip_run_id": str, "members": [{"change_id": str, "patchset": int, "sha": str}]}`') so writer and reader share a single contract.
findings.finding: The SHA-binding state file format and schema are unspecified. An executor must invent the structure (key names, nesting, encoding), risking divergence between the writer (S7/S8 assembly) and the reader (S8 pre-vote assertion).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'SHA-binding' AC
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the SHA-binding AC: 'The durable file is JSON at `<statedir>/batch-<batch_id>.json` with fields `batch_id` (str), `tip_run_id` (str), and `members` (array of `{change_id, patchset, sha}`); both S7's writer and S8's reader conform to this schema.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the default action for each run-outcome disagreement case in the 'Run-outcome confirmation' AC: (a) run_id mismatch while bridge vote is `+1` → treat as failure, abort, hand to S9; (b) bridge vote not observed within N minutes of run_id confirmation → timeout, abort, hand to S9; specify N (e.g. 10 minutes).
findings.finding: The run-outcome confirmation AC specifies that the bot asserts the observed GHA `run_id` matches the S7 handoff record AND that the bridge-cast Verified vote passes — but does not state what the bot does if these two signals disagree (e.g. bridge vote is `+1` but run_id doesn't match, or run_id matches but bridge vote hasn't arrived yet). The failure action is left to the executor to invent.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Run-outcome confirmation' AC

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that S6 (broken-checkered-tiger) explicitly commits to authoring a 'Security invariants section' in ADR-0042 covering the SHA-binding invariant and its test; if S6's scope does not include this section, S8 must either own the ADR addition itself or add an explicit cross-story AC to S6 mandating it.
findings.finding: The plan's security-invariants AC (AC6) asserts that 'ADR-0042 (created by S6 broken-checkered-tiger) Security invariants section records the SHA-binding invariant... alongside the soundness argument.' ADR-0042 does not exist anywhere in the repository (docs/adr/ tops out at 0041-*); it is an artifact S6 must yet author. The plan treats a non-existent document's internal section structure as a confirmed property, making the AC unverifiable as written.
findings.location: AC: Security-invariants artifact — 'ADR-0042 (created by S6 broken-checkered-tiger) Security invariants section records...'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to S8's AC6: 'If ADR-0042 does not yet contain a Security invariants section when S8 is implemented, S8 appends that section to ADR-0042 directly (the SHA-binding + soundness argument + test AC are S8-owned content).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Ground the `autolander-bot` Service User identity: either cite the provisioning script/story that creates it, or explicitly declare it as new work this story owns (including the `infra/gerrit/service-user.sh` invocation, SSM secret path, and ACL confirmation).
findings.finding: The plan refers to the bot as 'Service User `autolander-bot`' and states 'Verified-casting is restricted to the bot Service User ACL.' No `autolander-bot` identity exists anywhere in the codebase: `infra/gerrit/service-user.sh` provisions `rebar-review-bot`, `infra/gerrit/setup-ci-service-account.sh` provisions `rebar-ci-bot`. The `autolander-bot` name is unverifiable — no provisioning script, no ACL entry, no SSM path, no compose service references it.
findings.location: What section: 'Service User `autolander-bot`'; AC5: 'Verified-casting is restricted to the bot Service User ACL'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete file path for the durable SHA-binding map (e.g. `/var/lib/autolander/batch-state/<batch_id>.json` or `infra/autolander/state/`) and confirm which story (S7 or S8) is responsible for creating that directory and defining the file schema.
findings.finding: The SHA-binding AC requires the `{change_id → patchset_SHA}` map to be 'written durably to the autolander state dir' and read back before casting. No `autolander state dir` exists in the repository (`infra/autolander/` is absent), and the plan names no concrete file path for this durable store. This is an empirically checkable codebase claim (the directory/path either exists or doesn't).
findings.location: AC: SHA-binding — 'written durably to the autolander state dir (a file that survives crash/restart)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the SHA-binding AC: 'State is persisted to `<explicit path>/<batch_id>.json` (created by S7, read by S8); the file must be fsync-written before S7 hands off, and S8 must open it O_RDONLY and verify its HMAC/checksum before casting any vote.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the hallucinated `autolander-bot` identity with the real casting account (`rebar-ci-bot` or a new bot whose provisioning script, SSM path, and ACL membership are named explicitly in this plan).
findings.finding: `autolander-bot` Service User named as the casting identity does not exist anywhere in the repo: not in `infra/gerrit/setup-ci-service-account.sh`, `infra/gerrit/service-user.sh`, `infra/gerrit/project.config`, or any SSM/IAM configuration. The only Verified-casting Service User is `rebar-ci-bot`. The plan names a hallucinated bot identity.
findings.location: What section: 'bot (Service User `autolander-bot`) casts `Verified +1`'; AC: 'Verified-casting is restricted to the bot Service User ACL'
findings.suggested_fix: Either reuse `rebar-ci-bot` (the existing Service User with Verified-casting rights, already in the `Service Users` group) and call it out explicitly, or name a new bot and add an AC requiring `infra/gerrit/setup-autolander-bot.sh` (analogous to `setup-ci-service-account.sh`) plus the SSM credential path.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge that ADR-0042 is a future deliverable of S6 (not yet present) and either add an explicit blocking dependency on S6's ADR-0042 section being merged, or specify the minimal ADR-0042 section S8 must ADD if S6 hasn't shipped it.
findings.finding: `ADR-0042` does not exist in `docs/adr/` — the directory tops out at `0041-llm-review-carry-trivial-rebase.md` and `0041-llm-diagnostic-sanitization.md`. S8's security-invariants AC treats ADR-0042 as already created by S6 (`broken-checkered-tiger`), but it is absent from the repo, making it a named artifact that cannot be verified or built upon.
findings.location: AC: 'Security-invariants artifact: the ADR-0042 (created by S6 broken-checkered-tiger) Security invariants section records…'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **T1 — Unverified capability assertion (g2p race):** Add an AC (or extend the Security-invariants artifact) that explicitly resolves how the existing g2p `workflow_dispatch`-on-patchset-created hook is suppressed or overridden for batch-member patchsets so that independently-triggered per-member CI runs cannot race with or overwrite the bot's SHA-bound fan-out `Verified +1` cast.
findings.finding: The fan-out cast mechanic assumes the bot's directly-cast `Verified +1` on each member's post-assembly patchset will be the final authoritative vote, but the existing g2p hook fires `workflow_dispatch` on every `patchset-created` event — including the new patchsets S7 mints on each member during batch rebase assembly. Each member therefore gets its own independent `gerrit-verify.yaml` CI run (per the per-change concurrency group confirmed in docs/88ab-feature-branch-evidence.md and gerrit-verify.yaml), which will eventually cast its own `Verified` vote on that member. This independent per-member CI vote could race with, succeed after, or overwrite the bot's fan-out-cast `Verified +1` from the combined run. The plan neither acknowledges this interaction nor resolves it — it is an unverified capability assertion that the bot's direct cast is stable and won't be superseded.
findings.location: AC: Fan-out protocol + mid-run failure; AC: Run-outcome confirmation
findings.suggested_fix: One of: (a) add a pre-fan-out step that suppresses g2p dispatch for member patchsets (e.g., by setting a batch-member marker the g2p filter recognizes and skips); (b) document in ADR-0042 that the fan-out cast is the *last* write and must happen only after all per-member CI runs have reached a terminal state; or (c) use Gerrit's REST `ignore` flag or a label-score guard so the bot's `Verified +1` is the highest-precedence vote and no subsequent bot vote can downgrade it within the submit window.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the durable SHA-binding state file schema (format, field names, key type, file path/naming convention within the autolander state dir) so the writer, reader, crash-recovery path, and AC6 security test all share an unambiguous contract.
findings.finding: The durable '{change_id → patchset_SHA}' state file that S8 writes at assembly and reads back before voting (and on crash/restart) has no defined schema, file format, field vocabulary, or location within the 'autolander state dir'. An implementer must invent this contract.
findings.location: AC1 (SHA-binding)
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit schema block to AC1, e.g.: 'File: `{STATE_DIR}/batch-{batch_id}.json`; JSON object with top-level keys `batch_id` (str), `members` (list of `{change_id: str, change_number: int, patchset: int, sha: str}`), `combined_tip_sha` (str), `run_id` (str), `state` (enum: ASSEMBLING | AWAITING_CI | CASTING_VOTES | SUBMITTED | FAILED).' This makes the writer, reader, and test all reference a single shared definition.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Co-locate with AC3 a concrete reconciliation protocol specifying: which signal is the primary wait, how/whether the run_id is carried in or derivable from the Gerrit Verified vote message, timeout/deadline for the secondary check, and the action (abort → S9) when either signal is absent or mismatched.
findings.finding: AC3 specifies two distinct run-outcome confirmation signals — (a) the Verified vote cast by the gerrit-to-platform bridge on the tip change, and (b) the GHA run_id matching the one S7 dispatched — but provides no co-located reconciliation protocol: ordering, timeout between them, what happens if signal (a) arrives but run_id mismatches, or if run_id matches but signal (a) never arrives.
findings.location: AC3 (Run-outcome confirmation)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile AC6's requirement for a 'Security invariants section' in ADR-0042 with S6's actual scope: either add this section explicitly to S6's AC3, or create the section in a new S8-owned doc, or explicitly note S8 extends ADR-0042 with this section.
findings.finding: AC6 requires ADR-0042 to contain a 'Security invariants section' recording the SHA-binding invariant and its concrete test, but S6 (broken-checkered-tiger) — the story that owns ADR-0042 — does not list this section in its Acceptance Criteria. S6's AC3 specifies ADR-0042 shall record: auto-lander, agent contract + outcome enum, staged serial→batch plan, separation-of-concerns boundary. SHA-binding security invariants are absent from S6's scope.
findings.location: AC6 (Security-invariants artifact)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to S6's AC3: 'The ADR also includes a Security Invariants section covering SHA-binding (cast-Verified bound to tested combined-tip SHA), the no-copied-vote path, the bot-only ACL constraint, and the soundness argument (atomic submit ⇒ intermediate states never reach main).' Then remove the duplication from S8's AC6 and cross-reference.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define S9's intake protocol (or stub it as a named interface) in S8's AC4: what record S8 writes when handing off to S9, what fields it carries (failure reason, partial-vote state, batch_id, retry count), and where S9 is specified — or add S9 as a forward-reference dependency.
findings.finding: S9 is referenced twice in AC4 as a concrete handoff target ('hand the batch back via S9' for both vote-failure and run-failure paths), but no S9 story is linked as a dependency of S8, no S9 ticket is defined in the fetched data, and S9's intake protocol (what it receives, what format, what it does with a failed batch) is entirely unspecified.
findings.location: AC4 (Fan-out protocol + mid-run failure)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC requiring a test that injects a per-member vote failure mid-fan-out and asserts: (a) no subsequent members receive a Verified vote, and (b) no submit call is issued — covering the abort path of the fan-out protocol.
findings.finding: The fan-out abort behavior — 'on any per-member vote failure, abort — cast no submit' — is a critical new failure path but no AC requires a test verifying that (a) subsequent members receive no vote after one failure, and (b) no submit is issued; the behavior is only specified, not tested.
findings.location: AC: 'Fan-out protocol + mid-fan-out failure'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the 'Fan-out protocol + mid-fan-out failure' AC: 'A test injects a Gerrit SSH vote failure on member N and asserts members N+1…end receive no vote and submitWholeTopic is not called; a separate test asserts the run-failure path casts no votes at all.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC requiring a test that presents a Verified vote on the tip change with a run_id differing from the handoff record and asserts no fan-out votes are cast (run_id mismatch rejection path).
findings.finding: The run-outcome confirmation AC requires that the observed GHA run_id matches the dispatched run_id before fan-out, but no AC requires a test for the mismatch rejection case — a stale or mismatched run_id would allow votes to be cast off the wrong CI run.
findings.location: AC: 'Run-outcome confirmation'
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the Security-invariants artifact AC or the run-outcome confirmation AC with: 'A test asserts that when the observed run_id ≠ the dispatched run_id in the handoff record, the bot casts no Verified votes and does not submit.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC requiring a test that simulates a mid-fan-out crash (partial vote state in the durable file), restarts the bot, and asserts re-validation occurs before any further vote is cast and previously-cast votes are handled correctly (no double-submit, correct member ordering resumed).
findings.finding: The crash/restart re-validation path — 'on crash/restart the batch is re-validated against the stored map before any vote is cast' — has no required test, including the partial-cast scenario (crash after some members voted, restart should not re-vote already-voted members or cast votes with a stale state).
findings.location: AC: 'SHA-binding (new threat vector)' — restart clause

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the fan-out command in AC4 from `ssh gerrit review --verified +1 <change>,<patchset>` to `ssh gerrit review <change>,<patchset> --label Verified=+1` (the actual Gerrit SSH subcommand flag for label votes).
findings.finding: The fan-out AC names `ssh gerrit review --verified +1 <change>,<patchset>` as the vote-casting command, but the Gerrit SSH `gerrit review` subcommand does not have a `--verified` flag; the correct flag is `--label Verified=+1`. No usage of `--verified` appears anywhere in the codebase. Using the wrong flag will be silently accepted by ssh but will cast no vote (or produce an unrecognized-option error), breaking the entire fan-out protocol at runtime.
findings.location: AC: Fan-out protocol + mid-run failure — "loop `ssh gerrit review --verified +1 <change>,<patchset>`"
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `ssh gerrit review --verified +1 <change>,<patchset>` with `ssh -p 29418 autolander-bot@<GERRIT_HOST> gerrit review <change>,<patchset> --label Verified=+1` — matching the Gerrit SSH API as used by `lfreleng-actions/gerrit-review-action` internally.

findings.finding: The plan specifies no failure-path or invalid-input test criteria for the Verified-casting bot: there is no AC or test plan covering what happens when the combined-tip CI run failed, when the run result is stale or ambiguous, or when the specific run ID cannot be confirmed — all critical guards for a gate-touching mechanism.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Add ACs such as: 'Bot emits no Verified vote and logs a structured error when run status is non-passing, stale, or unresolvable'; 'A test (integration or contract) exercises each of these paths and asserts no Verified vote is cast.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit auth preconditions for `autolander-bot`'s direct SSH Verified-casting: specify (a) that `autolander-bot` has an SSH keypair provisioned (analogous to `rebar-ci-bot` in `setup-ci-service-account.sh`), (b) that it is placed in the `Service Users` ACL group (or given explicit `label-Verified` permission), and (c) that the bot process uses a pinned known-hosts entry for Gerrit :29418.
findings.finding: S8 introduces `autolander-bot` casting `Verified +1` directly via SSH as a new integration, but the plan states no auth preconditions: no SSH keypair provisioning for `autolander-bot` is specified, no ACL grant of `label-Verified` to `autolander-bot` (distinct from `rebar-ci-bot` which already has this) is documented, and no statement of how the bot process on EC2 reaches Gerrit :29418 with a known-hosts check. The existing `label-Verified` ACL is granted to the `Service Users` group via `rebar-ci-bot`'s account; whether `autolander-bot` is in `Service Users` or granted `label-Verified` is entirely unspecified.
findings.location: AC: `Verified`-casting is restricted to the bot Service User ACL; AC: Fan-out protocol

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the SHA-binding state file is written atomically (write to a `.tmp` sibling, fsync, then rename) and that the read-back path validates file integrity (e.g., checksum or schema check) before trusting the map, rejecting a corrupt file as a hard error that triggers S9.
findings.finding: The plan relies on the SHA-binding state file for crash/restart recovery but does not specify atomic-write semantics (e.g., write-to-temp-then-rename) or an integrity check (e.g., checksum, JSON schema validation) on read-back. A crash mid-write leaves a corrupt or partial file; the restart path that 're-validates against the stored map' would then operate on bad data, silently passing or erroneously rejecting members.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — SHA-binding bullet: 'written durably to the autolander state dir … On crash/restart the batch is re-validated against the stored map'
findings.suggested_fix: Write the map as `batch-<id>.sha.tmp`, fsync, rename to `batch-<id>.sha`; on read-back, verify the JSON parses fully and a stored checksum matches; if either check fails, treat it as a missing map and route to S9 re-batch rather than proceeding.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a fallback protocol for missing/corrupt state file on crash/restart (e.g., treat absent file as unrecoverable → discard batch, notify S9; treat corrupt file as unrecoverable → same), and specify atomic-write semantics (write-to-temp-then-rename) to minimize the corruption window.
findings.finding: AC1's crash/restart recovery path ('the batch is re-validated against the stored map before any vote is cast') specifies no fallback for the case where the stored state file is absent or corrupt — e.g., the crash occurred before the first write completed.
findings.location: AC1 (SHA-binding, crash/restart clause)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Require that the SHA-binding map file is written atomically (write to a `.tmp` file then `rename(2)`/`os.replace()`) so a crash mid-write never leaves a corrupt file visible to the re-validation path.
findings.finding: The plan says the map is 'written durably' but does not specify an atomic write mechanism (e.g., write-to-temp-then-rename). A crash mid-write leaves a corrupt or partial file; on restart, the re-validation step reads this corrupt file with undefined behavior — potentially passing, failing with an unhelpful error, or blocking the batch permanently.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — SHA-binding AC: 'written durably to the autolander state dir … On crash/restart the batch is re-validated against the stored map'
findings.suggested_fix: Write to `<filename>.tmp` then atomically rename to `<filename>`; on restart, treat any file not ending in the final name as incomplete and re-assemble the batch.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the post-crash / partial-fan-out recovery contract: either (a) the bot casts Verified 0 (reset) on all already-voted members before handing to S9, or (b) the fan-out loop is explicitly idempotent (Gerrit re-cast of the same score at the same patchset is a no-op and the loop continues), and document which invariant holds after S9 takes over.
findings.finding: The plan does not address the state after a crash or abort that occurs mid fan-out — i.e., after Verified +1 has been successfully cast on one or more members but before the atomic submit. The plan specifies abort-on-vote-failure (forward path) but not rollback of already-cast votes on crash, nor whether the resume path re-casts idempotently or errors out. This leaves an undefined intermediate persisted state where some changes carry a bot-cast Verified +1 with no corresponding submit.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Fan-out protocol bullet: 'on any per-member vote failure, abort — cast no submit — and hand the batch back via S9'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that on any fan-out abort, S8 retracts Verified votes already cast in this batch (sets `--verified 0` on each change that received a vote in this fan-out attempt) before handing the batch to S9, to prevent orphaned votes from satisfying the gate on a subsequent re-batch.
findings.finding: On a partial fan-out failure (votes 1..K succeed, vote K+1 fails), the plan says 'abort — cast no submit' and hand to S9. But Verified +1 votes already cast on changes 1..K remain on Gerrit. On re-batch (S9), those orphaned votes satisfy the per-change Verified gate independently of any new combined run, violating the SHA-binding invariant — a change could land with a Verified +1 that was bound to a *different* combined-tip SHA than the one the new batch tests.
findings.location: AC: Fan-out protocol + mid-run failure
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the fan-out AC: 'Before handing off to S9, loop in reverse chain order and cast `ssh gerrit review --verified 0 <change>,<patchset>` on every member that received a +1 in this attempt; only hand to S9 after all retractions succeed (or are confirmed no-op).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the cleanup protocol for already-cast Verified +1 votes when fan-out aborts mid-loop (e.g., cast Verified 0 on all successfully-voted members before handing to S9, or specify S9's responsibility to clear them), so the vote semantic remains sound.
findings.finding: AC4's fan-out abort rule ('on any per-member vote failure, abort — cast no submit') leaves Verified +1 votes already successfully cast on members 1..K stranded on Gerrit with no specified retraction or cleanup protocol when vote K+1 fails.
findings.location: AC4 (Fan-out protocol + mid-run failure)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC4: 'On abort, cast `Verified 0` (reset) on all members that received `+1` in the current fan-out attempt before handing to S9, so no stranded votes persist.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command (or named test invocation) to each AC that produces evidence of success — e.g. `pytest tests/test_sha_binding.py -v` for the SHA-binding rejection AC, `ssh gerrit review --help | grep verified` + a dry-run batch log for the fan-out AC, and `jq -e '.members | length > 0' <statedir>/<batch_id>.json` for the durable-file AC.
findings.finding: No AC has a concrete proving command that produces evidence of success — the plan describes behaviors ('the bot reads it back and asserts', 'loop ssh gerrit review', 'abort — cast no submit') but never specifies a verifiable command or artifact check. An executing agent cannot distinguish 'done' from 'plausibly implemented' for any criterion.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all AC bullets

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that exercises the full fan-out → atomic-submit path end-to-end against a live or staging Gerrit instance (e.g. a named integration test or a documented dry-run batch in the staging environment that confirms changes reach `Submitted` state and the topic is correctly set before submit).
findings.finding: The new code path (bot casts `Verified +1` on Gerrit + calls atomic submit via submitWholeTopic) is the risky cutover, but no AC requires an end-to-end exercise of that path against a live (or staging) Gerrit instance. The only test named is a unit test for SHA-binding rejection logic — which bypasses the actual Gerrit vote and submit path entirely.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Fan-out protocol + mid-run failure' and 'Atomicity mechanism' ACs

findings.finding: None of the four ACs has a concrete proving command or artifact-check; every criterion is stated as a prose goal with no described verification step, violating E6's requirement that every completion-relevant claim have a concrete proving command.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all four ACs)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the secret lifecycle for the `autolander-bot` SSH key / service account credential: specify the storage mechanism (e.g., a secrets manager or HSM-backed store), the injection method into the bot runtime (e.g., short-lived token, env-var from vault), and the rotation policy — and ensure none of these appear in plaintext in code, IaC, or logs.
findings.finding: The plan crosses a network boundary (outbound SSH to Gerrit for `gerrit review --verified +1`) using the `autolander-bot` Service User credential, but is entirely silent on the secret lifecycle of that credential: how the SSH private key or API token is stored, how it is injected into the bot's runtime, and whether a secrets manager or rotation policy is in place. On a boundary-crossing path that gates code submission to main, an unmanaged credential is a high-severity exposure.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Fan-out protocol + mid-run failure' bullet; 'Verified-casting is restricted to the bot Service User ACL' bullet

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add an explicit AC to S8 covering the CONTRIBUTING.md / runbook updates (making S8 self-contained for its doc obligations), or add a concrete acceptance-criterion update to S6's scope that names S8's semantic changes — and verify S6 is not already closed before delegating.
findings.finding: The plan delegates CONTRIBUTING.md / runbook doc-drift 'to S6's docs sweep', but S6 ('broken-checkered-tiger') is described as the story that *created* ADR-0042 — implying S6 was scoped and likely planned before S8's semantic change to `Verified` was defined. There is no mechanism to ensure S6's docs sweep will actually cover S8's changes (the new `Verified` meaning, the SHA-binding state file, the fan-out abort protocol). If S6 is already closed or its scope is fixed, these updates are stranded.
findings.location: Last AC bullet: 'The submitWholeTopic / runbook / CONTRIBUTING.md doc-drift from this semantic shift is handed to S6's docs sweep'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the delegation bullet with: 'S8 includes an AC: update CONTRIBUTING.md and the autolander runbook to reflect that per-change `Verified +1` now means tested as part of its landed batch, document the SHA-binding state file location, and describe the fan-out abort protocol. If S6's docs sweep is in flight, coordinate via a shared checklist item tracked in both stories.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the circular doc-assignment: either own the runbook/CONTRIBUTING.md updates directly in S8's ACs, or assign them to a dedicated trailing docs story (not the upstream S6) that explicitly depends on S8.
findings.finding: The plan assigns 'doc-drift' work (submitWholeTopic / runbook / CONTRIBUTING.md updates) to 'S6's docs sweep,' but S8 depends on S6 — meaning S6 must complete before S8. If S6 is already closed when S8 ships, this doc work has no execution home, creating a circular or stranded dependency that contradicts the plan's own sequencing.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — last bullet: 'The submitWholeTopic / runbook / CONTRIBUTING.md doc-drift from this semantic shift is handed to S6's docs sweep.'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the last AC with: 'Create a follow-on docs story (or own it here in S8) to update the runbook and CONTRIBUTING.md with the new Verified semantics; do not assign this work to the already-prerequisite S6.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the topic-name uniqueness scheme for batch topics (e.g. a UUID generated at S7 assembly and stored in the handoff record), state that topic assignment happens at S7 assembly time and is part of the durable handoff record, and confirm the bot verifies topic membership before issuing the submit.
findings.finding: The atomicity mechanism specifies that 'the bot sets a shared topic across all members of the single linear relation chain before submit,' but the plan does not state: (a) at which step the topic is set (S7 assembly, S8 pre-fan-out, or S8 pre-submit), (b) how topic-name uniqueness is guaranteed across two concurrent batches that might run simultaneously, or (c) whether the topic is part of the durable handoff record S7 writes. If topic-setting is done by S8 without a uniqueness guarantee, two concurrent batches could share a topic, causing `submitWholeTopic` to pull in changes from both batches atomically — submitting changes from the second batch that have not yet received their Verified fan-out.
findings.location: AC: Atomicity mechanism

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete mechanism for run_id extraction: either (a) name the Gerrit REST endpoint used to fetch vote messages on the tip change and the parsing logic to extract `run_id` from the embedded URL, or (b) propose an alternative (e.g. the handoff record itself carries the expected `run_id` and the bot verifies it against the GHA API's run status directly, bypassing the vote-message parse).
findings.finding: The run-outcome confirmation AC requires the bot to extract the GHA `run_id` from the Gerrit `Verified` vote the bridge casts on the tip change, and assert it matches the `run_id` in the S7 handoff record. The bridge vote (via `lfreleng-actions/gerrit-review-action`) embeds the run URL in the vote message (confirmed: gerrit-verify.yaml comment: "The action's vote message embeds THIS run's URL"), but no mechanism for the bot to parse that message, extract the `run_id`, and compare it to the handoff record is specified or exists in the codebase. This is a new integration surface with no precedent.
findings.location: AC: Run-outcome confirmation — "asserts the observed GHA `run_id` matches the one S7 dispatched for this batch (from the handoff record)"

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify how doc-drift from S8 is handed to S6's sweep when S6 is an already-completed upstream story — either reopen/extend S6 with an explicit sub-task, create a new doc story, or move the doc-update obligation into S8's own AC.
findings.finding: The last AC delegates 'submitWholeTopic / runbook / CONTRIBUTING.md doc-drift' to 'S6's docs sweep,' but S6 (broken-checkered-tiger) is an upstream story that must be complete before S7 and S8 run (implied by the dependency chain S6→S7→S8). Handing work back to an already-completed story contradicts the sequential dependency ordering — there is no mechanism described for how a completed story's sweep absorbs new scope from a later story.
findings.location: Last AC bullet: 'The submitWholeTopic / runbook / CONTRIBUTING.md doc-drift from this semantic shift is handed to S6's docs sweep.'
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) add a concrete AC to S8 that performs the CONTRIBUTING.md/runbook updates directly, or (b) create a dedicated follow-on story for the doc sweep and reference it by name, making clear S6 is not the owner.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: The plan's soundness argument 'atomic submit ⇒ intermediate states never reach main' (Why section) is an unverified assertion about the batching mechanism's Gerrit topology. The existing `submitWholeTopic = true` + FFO (ADR-0040) provides atomic submit only for a *linear topic*. The plan never asserts, and no codebase artifact confirms, that all batch members will share a single Gerrit topic — which is the required condition for `submitWholeTopic` to submit them atomically. Without this, the 'intermediate states never reach main' soundness argument has an unbacked premise.
findings.location: Why section: 'ancestor-atomic submit means the untested intermediate states never reach `main`'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan for the Verified semantic change — describe how to revert Gerrit ACLs, bot behavior, and documentation to per-change CI semantics if batching is disabled or this story is reverted.
findings.finding: The plan changes the observable semantics of an existing gate (Verified +1 now means 'tested as part of its landed batch' rather than 'tested in isolation'), which is a behavioral interface change that consumers — reviewers, audit systems, dashboards, compliance tooling — may rely on. The plan acknowledges the change but provides no rollback/back-out plan or expand-contract sequencing for reverting to per-change CI semantics if the batching approach is abandoned or the feature is disabled.
findings.location: AC bullet: 'Verified-casting is restricted to the bot Service User ACL; documented that per-change Verified now means tested as part of its landed batch, not tested in isolation.' / Last AC: 'doc-drift... handed to S6's docs sweep.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' section or AC describing: (1) how to disable the fan-out bot and restore direct per-change Verified voting, (2) any ACL changes needed, and (3) a flag or feature toggle the bot checks before casting batched Verified votes, so the semantic shift can be cleanly reversed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a RETIRE/cleanup path for SHA-binding map files: specify when they are deleted or archived (e.g., on successful submit or after a TTL), and document this in the state-dir lifecycle.
findings.finding: The SHA-binding map file has no defined RETIRE/cleanup path. After a batch successfully submits, the durable state file is never mentioned as being deleted or archived, so files accumulate in the autolander state dir indefinitely.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — SHA-binding AC: 'written durably to the autolander state dir'
findings.suggested_fix: After ancestor-atomic submit succeeds, delete or move the batch's map file to an archive directory. Use a per-batch filename (e.g., `<batch_id>-sha-map.json`) so files are distinct and cleanup is unambiguous.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the file naming convention for SHA-binding map files (e.g., `<batch_id>-sha-map.json`) and document that each batch gets an isolated file, ensuring no cross-batch clobbering.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify the file naming scheme for the SHA-binding map, leaving concurrency ownership ambiguous: if two batches are in flight simultaneously (e.g., one being voted and one being assembled), a shared or colliding filename could cause one batch's map to overwrite or corrupt the other's.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — SHA-binding AC: 'written durably to the autolander state dir'
findings.suggested_fix: Name each file after its batch identifier (e.g., Gerrit topic or a UUID assigned at assembly) so concurrent batches never share a filename.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an observability requirement: each S8 failure path (SHA-binding rejection, run_id mismatch, per-member vote failure, abort-to-S9) must emit a structured log entry and increment a named counter/metric observable by operators.
findings.finding: No observability instrumentation is specified for the new failure points introduced by S8: vote cast failures, SHA-binding rejections, run_id mismatches, and abort-to-S9 events. Operators have no stated way to detect or diagnose these failures.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Fan-out protocol, SHA-binding, Run-outcome confirmation ACs

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define an explicit, versioned schema (field names, types, format) for the S7→S8 handoff record and the durable autolander state-dir file, and reference it from both S7 and S8 so the cross-component contract is explicit and independently evolvable.
findings.finding: The S7→S8 handoff record — which carries both the `run_id` S7 dispatched and the `{change_id→patchset_SHA}` map — is a cross-component contract with no specified schema, field names, or versioning. S8 reads `run_id` from 'the handoff record' and the SHA map from 'the autolander state dir', but the plan never specifies whether these are the same artifact, their format, or what happens if S7 evolves its output. If S7 changes its record structure, S8's SHA-binding and run-id matching silently break.
findings.location: AC: 'Run-outcome confirmation' and 'SHA-binding' bullets; 'Dependencies' section referencing S7
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC bullet: 'The handoff record and the SHA-binding state file conform to a named, versioned JSON schema (e.g. `autolander/schemas/batch_handoff_v1.json`) checked into the repo; both S7 and S8 reference it; a schema-validation step runs at read time.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a retry-with-backoff policy for individual SSH gerrit review vote calls (e.g., 3 attempts with exponential backoff) before treating a per-member vote failure as terminal and aborting to S9.
findings.finding: The fan-out protocol specifies 'on any per-member vote failure, abort' with no retry or backoff before aborting. A transient SSH connectivity blip or a momentary Gerrit overload causes an immediate batch abort and re-queue to S9, even though a single retry would likely succeed.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Fan-out protocol + mid-run failure: 'on any per-member vote failure, abort — cast no submit'
findings.suggested_fix: Retry each `ssh gerrit review --verified +1` call up to 3 times with 1s/2s/4s backoff; only abort to S9 if all retries are exhausted. Log each retry attempt.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the concrete file(s) S8 will create or modify (e.g., `infra/autolander/cast_verified.py` for the fan-out+vote loop, and the state-dir path constant) so the edit set is verifiable.
findings.finding: S8 names no concrete implementation file or module as its edit target. The plan describes significant new behavior (SHA-binding state write/read, fan-out vote loop, run-outcome confirmation, abort-on-failure logic) but specifies no file path (e.g., `infra/autolander/cast_verified.py` or similar) where this code lives. Combined with the fact that `infra/autolander/` does not yet exist in the repo, the plan's edit set is entirely absent.
findings.location: What section; AC: 'SHA-binding', 'Fan-out protocol', 'Run-outcome confirmation'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `file_impact` entry such as `infra/autolander/cast_verified.py` (new: SHA-binding read, fan-out vote loop, run-outcome confirmation) and specify the absolute path or env-variable name for the durable state directory.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete path for the autolander state directory (e.g., `/var/lib/autolander/` or `AUTOLANDER_STATE_DIR` env var) and confirm it is created by S7 or by S8's own setup step.
findings.finding: The plan references `infra/autolander/` (via the S7 dependency's `batch.py` and S8's own 'autolander state dir') as if the directory will exist when S8 runs, but no story in the dependency chain is confirmed to have created it — S7 names `infra/autolander/batch.py` as a net-new file but its story is still `open` and the directory is wholly absent from the repo. S8 has no AC requiring the directory to be created/confirmed before vote-casting code is added.
findings.location: AC: 'written durably to the autolander state dir (a file that survives crash/restart)'; Dependencies section references S7

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the access posture of the inbound run-outcome signal: specify which Gerrit ACL (restricted to the bridge's own Service User account, not the general Verified label permission) governs the `Verified` vote the bot trusts, and document that the bot cross-checks the vote's author identity against the expected bridge service account before treating it as a confirmed pass.
findings.finding: The inbound trust signal — the `Verified` vote the gerrit-to-platform bridge casts on the tip change, which the bot reads as run-outcome confirmation — has an undeclared access posture. The plan does not specify which Gerrit ACL governs who may cast this inbound Verified vote, leaving open whether a lower-trust actor (e.g., any user with Verified+1 permission on the tip change, or the bridge acting on a spoofed webhook payload) could inject a fraudulent passed-run signal. The run_id assertion is a partial guard but is only as strong as the trust anchor for the run_id value itself; if the inbound Verified vote is spoofable, the run_id embedded in it may also be attacker-controlled.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Run-outcome confirmation' bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC sub-bullet under 'Run-outcome confirmation': 'The bot validates that the inbound `Verified +1` on the tip was cast by the designated bridge Service User account (not any holder of the Verified label permission); the bridge Service User ACL is restricted to the bridge identity only. The bot rejects any run-outcome signal not authored by that identity, regardless of run_id.'

findings.finding: The plan asserts 'no copied-vote path' as a security property (AC1, AC3), treating this as covered by the existing GerriScary/copyCondition posture — but the actual threat in this story is an EXPLICIT bot-cast Verified+1 on non-tip batch members (changes that were NOT the tip the combined CI run built against). The existing copyCondition=NO_CODE_CHANGE guard prevents *carried* stale votes, not *directly cast* votes. No codebase artifact (project.config, ADR-0020, ADR-0023, gerrit-verify.yaml) implements or audits a guard against the bot mistakenly casting Verified+1 on a non-tip member using a run ID that tested a different SHA. These are architecturally distinct threat vectors.
findings.location: AC1 ('never a copied or stale vote — GerriScary posture') and AC3 ('no copied-vote path')
findings.suggested_fix: In AC1, replace 'never a copied or stale vote — GerriScary posture' with: 'The bot MUST verify that the patchset SHA of each batch member it votes on matches the SHA that was included in the combined-tip run (e.g., by recording [change-id, patchset-SHA] at batch-assembly time and asserting identity before casting). The GerriScary/copyCondition guard covers carried votes; this binding check covers directly-cast votes on non-tip members — a distinct and new threat vector.'

findings.finding: The plan asserts the bot casts `Verified +1` on each batch member after the combined-tip CI passes, but the repo's proven `Verified`-casting path (ADR-0023, `gerrit-verify.yaml`) issues one SSH vote per patchset per CI run — one change, one run, one vote. Casting `Verified +1` on N non-tip batch members from a single combined-tip run is a novel multi-target vote mechanic that has no verified precedent in the codebase. The plan does not identify this as novel, cite any Gerrit API capability that supports a single run voting on multiple distinct change-numbers, or acknowledge the `copyCondition = NO_CODE_CHANGE` implication (any rebase that assembled the combined chain would have minted new patchsets, dropping prior `Verified` votes).
findings.location: What section: 'bot casts `Verified +1` on each batch member'; AC1: 'casts Verified +1 on batch members ONLY after confirming the specific combined-tip run passed'
findings.suggested_fix: The ADR addendum (AC4) should include: (a) the `gerrit review -m … --verified +1 <change-id>,<patchset>` invocation pattern for N members, (b) confirmation that each invocation targets the post-assembly patchset number (not a stale one), and (c) a statement that this is N independent fresh votes (not a copy), satisfying ADR-0020's `copyCondition = NO_CODE_CHANGE` requirement.

findings.finding: The plan invokes 'GerriScary posture' as a named security invariant in AC1 ('never a copied or stale vote — GerriScary posture') and AC3 ('no copied-vote path'), but the actual threat in this story is bot-programmatic vote fan-out (the bot intentionally casting Verified +1 on changes whose trees the combined-tip CI run did not directly test), which is orthogonal to the existing GerriScary/copyCondition mechanism. The plan never defines the concrete invariant that maps the GerriScary brand to this new threat model, creating an instruction-locality gap.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 ('GerriScary posture'), AC3 ('no copied-vote path')

findings.finding: The plan asserts 'submits atomically' and 'ancestor-atomic submit' but never specifies which Gerrit mechanism delivers that atomicity. The two deployed candidates — `submitWholeTopic=true` (requires all changes share a topic AND be linearizable) and FFO relation-chain submit — have different topological requirements, and ADR-0040 explicitly warns that 'a single topic containing parallel sibling changes to `main` cannot be linearized and its atomic submit fails.' If Phase 1's batch assembly produces a topology that doesn't match the mechanism assumed here (e.g., parallel-sibling topic vs. linear relation-chain), the 'atomic submit' step will fail silently or loudly at runtime rather than at design time.
findings.location: Why section ('ancestor-atomic submit means the untested intermediate states never reach `main`'); What section ('then submits atomically'); AC3 ('the two-vote gate is not weakened')
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the 'What' section: 'Atomicity is delivered by Gerrit's `submitWholeTopic=true` (server config, already deployed) on a shared topic whose changes form a linear relation-chain — the topology Phase 1's batch-assembly story guarantees. Parallel-sibling topologies are excluded (per ADR-0040 Wikimedia limitation). The bot sets the topic on all batch members before submitting.'

findings.finding: The plan specifies that the bot casts `Verified +1` ONLY after 'confirming the specific combined-tip run passed,' but the interface/contract between the CI system and the bot — the mechanism by which the bot learns a specific run's outcome — is unspecified. This is a new cross-component dependency introduced without an explicit event, API, or data-contract boundary.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — item 1: 'confirms the specific combined-tip run passed'; Why/What sections describing the bot's behavior

findings.finding: AC1 references 'confirming the specific combined-tip run passed' as a decision rule but never specifies the confirmation protocol: the existing `gerrit-verify.yaml` is dispatched per patchset-created and votes on the triggering patchset; no mechanism in the plan or codebase defines how the bot identifies, polls, or asserts a specific combined-tip run's conclusion before fan-out voting. This is a processing-protocol referenced without co-located specification.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1: 'confirming the specific combined-tip run passed'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC1: 'Confirmation is by matching the GitHub Actions run_id (or equivalent immutable run handle) recorded at dispatch time against the run whose conclusion is being read; the bot stores this binding atomically with the dispatch and re-verifies it before casting any vote.'

findings.finding: The plan says the bot 'casts Verified +1 on each batch member' but never specifies the fan-out voting protocol: which Gerrit API or SSH command, in what order, what the loop termination condition is, and how the bot knows which patchset number is current for each batch member at vote time. The existing `gerrit-verify.yaml` votes only on the patchset that triggered it; there is no fan-out vote path in the codebase.
findings.location: What section: 'the bot casts Verified +1 on each batch member'; Acceptance Criteria — AC2

findings.finding: AC3 asserts 'the two-vote gate is not weakened' as a security invariant but specifies no observable, end-state proof mechanism — no test, CI check, audit-log assertion, or formal review sign-off is named that would confirm this invariant holds after the change.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'the two-vote gate is not weakened'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC or sub-step: 'A scripted or manual ACL check confirms that only the bot Service User account can cast Verified votes; this check is run as part of the security audit and its output is attached to the ADR addendum.'

findings.finding: The plan specifies no fallback for an incomplete or failed sub-step in the Verified-casting sequence: if the combined-tip CI run passes but one or more fan-out `Verified +1` casts fail (SSH error, patchset-not-current, network timeout), no recovery path is defined. The plan also omits what happens if the combined-tip CI run itself fails — should the bot cast `Verified -1` on all batch members, on none, or on only the tip?
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1, AC3; What section

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
